message.content: I am writing a new Python script to scrape Project Gutenberg e-books. I will need to perform several network and file operations: 1. Fetching content from an external URL (e.g., using 'requests'). 2. Parsing HTML content (using BeautifulSoup). 3. Downloading the final file content and saving it locally. Please identify any existing utilities, functions, or examples in the codebase that handle these tasks, specifically focusing on network calls, request handling, and structured data extraction from HTML. Report file paths and relevant code snippets.

content.text: There's an issue with the selected model (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001). It may not exist or you may not have access to it. Run /model to pick a different model.
